codeReview.summary: The health-endpoint feature implementation correctly addresses all three expected behaviors: (1) GET /health returns 200 with {"status":"ok"}, (2) Response includes version information ("2.3.20"), and (3) Response includes auth status ("authenticated" or "not_authenticated"). The implementation is straightforward and uses verifyCursorAuth() from auth.ts to determine authentication state.

issues.description: Version "2.3.20" is hardcoded rather than dynamically read from package.json. The handoff stated the version should come from package.json but it was hardcoded. While the current value matches package.json correctly, this creates a maintenance risk - if package.json version changes, this hardcoded value will be stale. Consider reading version dynamically using require('../package.json').version at module load time.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker's skill "worker" was not found during transcript execution. The transcript shows 'Error: Skill "worker" not found', but the feature specifies skillName: "worker". This didn't block execution but indicates the skill may not be properly configured in .factory/skills/.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows: '## Tool Result\nError: Skill "worker" not found'

summary: Feature implementation passes review. All expected behaviors are correctly implemented: HTTP 200 response with {"status":"ok","version":"2.3.20","auth":"authenticated"} or {"auth":"not_authenticated"}. One non-blocking issue noted: hardcoded version instead of dynamic package.json read.
